summarization.a: The figure shows absorbance spectra at different energies for various materials (LiMn2O4 ref, Mn2O3 ref, MnO2 ref, 200cLi-MnO2 and 200cLi-MnO2-600).
summarization.b: The figure shows absorbance spectra at different energies for various materials (LiMn2O4 ref, Mn2O3 ref, MnO2 ref, 300cLi-MnO2 and 300cLi-MnO2-600).
summarization.c: The figure shows the absorbance of different materials (LiMn2O4 ref, Mn2O3 ref, MnO2 ref, 200cLi-MnO2 and 200cLi-MnO2-600) as a function of distance R.
summarization.d: The figure shows the absorbance of different materials (LiMn2O4 ref, Mn2O3 ref, MnO2 ref, 300cLi-MnO2 and 300cLi-MnO2-600) as a function of distance R.
summarization.e: The figure shows absorbance spectra at different energies for different oxidation states of Mn (Mn+3 and Mn+4).
summarization.f: The figure shows absorbance spectra at different energies for different oxidation states of Mn (Mn+3 and Mn+4).

f.question: What effect does the annealing treatment have on the samples?
f.answer: It seems that the annealing treatment removes the Mn+3 peak from the spectra.

d.question: Which sample has the highest intensity of the Mg-O bond?

a.question: Which samples were studied and shown in the absorbance spectra?
a.answer: LiMnO2O4, Mn2O3, and MnO2 films used as a reference. Also, 200cLi-MnO2, 300cLi-MnO2, and 200cLi-MnO2-600 without annealing treatment;  and 300cLi-MnO2-600, with annealing treatment.

b.question: What effect does the annealing treatment have on the 300cLi-MnO2 sample?
b.answer: The annealing treatment produces a shift in the peak to a higher energy values.
